mnemonics: Finalizers are for Cleanup, Not for Resurrection (Avoid bringing objects back from the dead)
keyPoints: Garbage Collection is automatic memory management for reclaiming unused objects.
An object becomes eligible for GC when it is unreachable (no live thread holds a reference to it).
The `System.gc()` method is a hint to the JVM, not a guarantee that collection will run.
commonMistakes: Relying on finalize() method for critical resource cleanup (use try-with-resources instead).
Creating memory leaks by holding unnecessary object references (e.g., in static fields or collections).
